steps.title: Spot the author's deliberate caveat
steps.content: The strongest clue is the parenthetical: “although in Hans Castorp's favor it should be noted that it is his story, and that not every story happens to everybody.” Despite emphasizing the story is told “not for his sake,” the author credits Hans Castorp for being the subject of this remarkable story.

steps.title: Match the caveat to the answer choices
steps.content:
Choice C captures the crucial logic: “because the story is valuable, some of its value accrues to the person at its center.” This balances both the dismissal of Hans Castorp as unimportant and the recognition that he deserves credit for bearing such a remarkable story.

• Choice B correctly identifies the person as unimportant and sees the story's importance in its age and manner of telling, but overlooks the explicit credit given to the person.

Choices A and D misrepresent or overlook this tension. That matches choice C — C is correct.
